findings.location: s1 — radioButtons / checkboxes HTML output examples
findings.description: The source includes rendered HTML output for n:radioButtons and n:checkboxes showing the ID naming convention (nablarch_radio1, nablarch_radio2, … / nablarch_checkbox1, …), the label/input/br structure, and how checked state is rendered. These are omitted from the knowledge file. An AI asked 'what HTML does n:radioButtons generate?' or helping a user write CSS/JS selectors targeting these generated IDs would lack this information.
findings.source_evidence:
Source radioButtons HTML output: `` `
` ... and checkboxes HTML: `` etc.

findings.location: s4 — encryption: distinct purposes of requestId vs hash value
findings.description: The source explains that the request ID and the hash value serve different distinct tamper-detection purposes: the request ID detects cross-form value swapping (暗号化した値を入れ替えた場合の改竄), while the hash value detects in-place value modification (値の書き換えによる改竄). The knowledge file only says '改竄検知用ハッシュ値を含め' without distinguishing these two mechanisms.
findings.source_evidence: Source: 'リクエストIDは、異なる入力フォーム間で暗号化した値を入れ替えた場合の改ざんを検知するために、ハッシュ値は、値の書き換えによる改竄を検知するために使用する。'
